settings.hero_heading: Build a routine that works with your skin.
settings.hero_intro: A great skincare routine doesn't need to be complicated. It needs to be consistent, well-ordered, and matched to your skin. We'll help you build one.
settings.support_heading: Two essentials that support every serum.
settings.support_intro: Our Barrier Cream and Facial Spray are designed to work alongside all eight serums — sealing in actives and preparing skin for maximum absorption.
settings.cream_desc: The final step in every routine. Ceramide NP and Shea Butter seal in your serum's active ingredients and prevent overnight moisture loss. Apply a pea-sized amount after your serum has fully absorbed.
settings.spray_desc: Applied before your serum on slightly damp skin, the facial spray prepares the surface to maximise active ingredient penetration. Especially effective before Hyaluronic Acid and Vitamin C serums.
settings.mistakes_heading: Mistakes to avoid when building your routine.
settings.cta_heading: Let us build your routine for you.
settings.cta_body: Answer five questions and we'll recommend the right serums for your skin goals, type, and experience with active ingredients.

settings.name: Facial Spray (optional)
settings.description: Mist lightly on a clean face to prepare the skin surface. Creates a slightly damp base that enhances absorption of the next step.

settings.name: Serum
settings.description: Apply 3–5 drops to fingertips and press gently into skin. Allow 60 seconds to absorb before moving to the next step.

settings.name: Barrier Cream
settings.description: Apply a pea-sized amount to seal in your serum and lock in moisture. Works on every skin type including oily and combination.

settings.name: SPF 30+
settings.description: Non-negotiable, especially if using Vitamin C or active acids. Apply generously as the final morning step after the Barrier Cream has absorbed.

settings.name: Double Cleanse
settings.description: Start with an oil cleanser to dissolve sunscreen and makeup. Follow with a gentle water-based cleanser. This thorough approach prevents congestion.

settings.name: Serum
settings.description: Apply your PM serum (or alternate between two on separate evenings if using exfoliants and Retinol). Allow full absorption before barrier cream.

settings.name: Barrier Cream
settings.description: The most important evening step. Seals in active ingredients while your skin's overnight repair mechanisms are active. Apply as the final step.

settings.title: Hydration Routine
settings.description: For dehydrated skin that feels tight or looks dull. Focus on layering humectants before sealing with the Barrier Cream to lock in moisture.

settings.title: Brightening Routine
settings.description: For uneven skin tone, dark spots, and dullness. Vitamin C works best in the morning, paired with SPF to protect against UV-induced melanin production.

settings.title: Barrier Repair Routine
settings.description: For reactive, sensitive, or compromised skin. Strip back to the essentials and let the barrier rebuild before introducing stronger actives.

settings.title: Resurfacing Routine
settings.description: For rough texture, enlarged pores, and congestion. Use the Smooth Resurfacing Serum every other evening to avoid over-exfoliation. Always follow with Barrier Cream.

settings.title: Calming Routine
settings.description: For persistent redness, rosacea, and reactive skin. The Calm Serum can be used twice daily. Avoid harsh exfoliants and introduce any new active slowly.

settings.title: Glow Routine
settings.description: For luminosity and that healthy, dewy look. Bakuchiol in the Glow Elixir delivers Retinol-like renewal without the sensitivity risk — making it suitable morning and evening.

settings.title: Hydra-Boost + Barrier Repair
settings.note: Both use AM + PM timing. Apply Hydra-Boost first (water-thin texture), then Barrier Repair Serum. Seal with Barrier Cream. Ideal for chronically dry or compromised skin.

settings.title: Bright-C + Glow Elixir
settings.note: Vitamin C in the morning, Bakuchiol-powered Glow Elixir in the evening. Two complementary brightening mechanisms without any conflict — and no photosensitivity risk.

settings.title: Age-Defy + Hydra-Boost
settings.note: Retinol on alternate evenings, Hydra-Boost every morning. Retinol can cause initial dryness — daily hydration with Hyaluronic Acid counteracts this and supports skin through the adjustment period.

settings.title: Clear Skin + Smooth Resurfacing
settings.note: Use Clear Skin Serum daily (AM + PM), and add Smooth Resurfacing Serum two to three times per week in the evening. Never use both on the same night.

settings.title: Layering incompatible actives
settings.description: Mixing AHAs/BHAs with Retinol in the same routine causes significant irritation. Use exfoliating acids and Retinol on alternate evenings — they'll both work better for it.

settings.title: Skipping SPF with actives
settings.description: Vitamin C, AHAs, BHAs, and Retinol all increase photosensitivity. Without SPF, you're undoing the benefits and risking pigmentation. SPF 30 minimum, every morning.

settings.title: Introducing too many new products at once
settings.description: If your skin reacts, you'll have no way to identify the cause. Introduce one new product per two-week period so you can accurately assess tolerance.

settings.title: Not waiting for absorption
settings.description: Applying moisturiser immediately after serum dilutes the active ingredients before they can absorb. Give each product 60–90 seconds to penetrate before applying the next.

settings.title: Over-exfoliating
settings.description: More exfoliation does not mean faster results. Using acids or Retinol every night, especially at the start, breaks down the skin barrier and triggers inflammation. Every other night is the correct frequency.

settings.title: Abandoning a routine too early
settings.description: Meaningful skin changes take 6–12 weeks of consistent use. Switching products before this window closes means you'll never know what was actually working — or what wasn't.
